articles.title: I wrote about George Santos. Then he made a violent threat and lied about it
articles.summary: NPR reported on new federal investigations examining the former Congressman's bets on the prediction market site Kalshi. Then he threatened the NPR reporter who broke the story.
articles.titleZh: 我写过关于乔治·桑托斯的文章。然后他发出了暴力威胁并撒了谎
articles.summaryZh: NPR 报道了新的联邦调查，该调查审查了前国会议员在预测市场网站 Kalshi 上的赌注。然后他威胁了揭露此事的 NPR 记者。

contentParagraphs.en: Former U.S. Rep. George Santos is being investigated for trades on the prediction market Kalshi where he appears to have profited by deceiving the public about attending Trump's State of the Union address in February.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国前众议员乔治·桑托斯 (George Santos) 因在 Kalshi 预测市场上进行交易而受到调查，他似乎通过欺骗公众参加特朗普 2 月份的国情咨文演讲而获利。

contentParagraphs.en: Julia Demaree Nikhinson/AP/AP
contentParagraphs.zh: 朱莉娅·德玛丽·尼金森/美联社/美联社

contentParagraphs.en: Julia Demaree Nikhinson/AP/AP
contentParagraphs.zh: 朱莉娅·德玛丽·尼金森/美联社/美联社

contentParagraphs.en: I was winding down my work day here in Los Angeles when my phone rang at 5:37 p.m. from a blocked number. It was former Congressman George Santos. He was boiling with rage.
contentParagraphs.zh: 下午 5 点 37 分，我正在洛杉矶结束一天的工作，这时我的电话响了。来自被阻止的号码。他就是前国会议员乔治·桑托斯。他怒不可遏。

contentParagraphs.en: The day before, I published a story revealing that the Justice Department and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission had opened investigations into his trading activity on the prediction market site Kalshi.
contentParagraphs.zh: 前一天，我发表了一篇报道，透露司法部和商品期货交易委员会已对他在预测市场网站 Kalshi 上的交易活动展开调查。

contentParagraphs.en: Officials at the company detected that he was betting against his appearance at Trump's State of the Union address in February, just as he posted a video on X gushing to his followers about how excited he was to attend. With the help of three sources, I was able to confirm that Kalshi referred the matter to federal authorities in the Southern District of New York and Washington.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该公司的官员发现，他在押注自己不会出现在 2 月份的特朗普国情咨文演讲中，当时他在 X 上发布了一段视频，向他的追随者滔滔不绝地表达他对出席会议的兴奋之情。在三个消息来源的帮助下，我得以确认卡尔希将此事提交给了纽约南区和华盛顿的联邦当局。

contentParagraphs.en: Before the story, I emailed him, and he called me from a blocked number. So when my phone buzzed again from a blocked number, I had a pretty good sense of who it might be.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在报道之前，我给他发了电子邮件，他用一个被屏蔽的号码给我打电话。因此，当我的手机因被屏蔽的号码再次响起时，我很清楚可能是谁。

contentParagraphs.en: Santos, whose political rise and fall was characterized by a notorious trail of lies and falsehoods, claimed my story was riddled with errors. He said "my lawyers have been calling the Department of Justice all day, and they can't find any investigation."
contentParagraphs.zh: 桑托斯的政治兴衰以其臭名昭著的谎言和谎言为特征，他声称我的故事充满了错误。他说：“我的律师整天给司法部打电话，但他们找不到任何调查。”

contentParagraphs.en: As we were talking, I asked if I could record the call. He said no. I was in front of a keyboard, though, furiously jotting down every word.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当我们谈话时，我问是否可以录音。他说不。然而，我正坐在键盘前，疯狂地记下每一个字。

contentParagraphs.en: I asked him who his lawyers are, and he refused to answer. I questioned whether he really does have attorneys. He replied: "I'm George f*cking Santos, of course I have a legal team."
contentParagraphs.zh: 我问他的律师是谁，他拒绝回答。我质疑他是否真的有律师。他回答道：“我是乔治桑托斯，当然我有一个法律团队。”

contentParagraphs.en: He then proceeded to name-call and attack the reputation of NPR, the kind of invective that's common when reporting on people who try to discredit reporters and news organizations for stories they don't like.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然后，他开始辱骂并攻击 NPR 的声誉，这种谩骂在报道那些试图因为记者和新闻机构不喜欢的报道而抹黑记者和新闻机构的人时很常见。

contentParagraphs.en: What Santos said next took me aback, even by his outlandish and brazen standards.
contentParagraphs.zh: 桑托斯接下来说的话让我大吃一惊，即使按照他古怪而厚颜无耻的标准来看也是如此。

contentParagraphs.en: "This story is going to get you a gun in your face," Santos said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “这个故事会拿枪指着你的脸，”桑托斯说。

contentParagraphs.en: I asked him what he meant by that.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我问他这是什么意思。

contentParagraphs.en: "You know what I mean."
contentParagraphs.zh: “你知道我的意思。”

contentParagraphs.en: It did not exactly feel like an imminent threat to my life that a convicted fraudster expelled from Congress who lives thousands of miles away from me in Pennsylvania's Pocono Mountains was lodging violent words at me.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一位住在距我数千英里之外宾夕法尼亚州波科诺山脉的被定罪的欺诈者向我发出暴力言语，并被国会开除，这并不完全像是对我生命的迫在眉睫的威胁。

contentParagraphs.en: It felt more bizarre than threatening, but then it grew even stranger and more confusing.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这感觉比威胁更奇怪，但随后变得更奇怪、更令人困惑。

contentParagraphs.en: While he had been calling me from a blocked number, I tracked down his cell phone from a public records search, and I sent him a text message confirming it was him. He greeted it by launching into full-throated denial. "I NEVER SAID 'this story would get a gun in your face, I said 'it'd blow up in your face," he wrote in a text.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当他用被屏蔽的号码给我打电话时，我通过公共记录搜索找到了他的手机，并向他发送了一条短信，确认是他本人。他对此表示断然否认。他在短信中写道：“我从来没有说过‘这个故事会用枪指着你的脸，我说的是‘它会在你的脸上爆炸’。”

contentParagraphs.en: He then called me "an insane person," "a clown," among other broadsides.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然后他称我为“一个疯子”、“一个小丑”等等。

contentParagraphs.en: Santos was front-running his denial of his violent threat before I ever publicly confronted him for doing it.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在我公开质问桑托斯之前，他就否认了自己的暴力威胁。

contentParagraphs.en: Not long after, he took to his favorite megaphone, X, to tell the world this never happened, claiming I "was now making things up," even though I had yet to publicly reveal what he had said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 不久之后，他用他最喜欢的扩音器 X 告诉全世界这件事从未发生过，并声称我“现在正在编造事情”，尽管我还没有公开透露他所说的内容。

contentParagraphs.en: "I've interacted with hundreds of reporters in my life… not once was I ever threatening or aggressive… sassy? Sure but aggressive and threatening? NEVER!" he wrote.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我一生中与数百名记者互动过……我从来没有威胁过或具有攻击性……粗鲁？当然，但具有攻击性和威胁性？从来没有！”他写道。

contentParagraphs.en: In the post, he added: "He's now demanding I disclose the names of my lawyers 'or else' (only God knows what that means.)"
contentParagraphs.zh: 他在帖子中补充道：“他现在要求我透露我律师的姓名，‘否则’（只有上帝知道这意味着什么。）”

contentParagraphs.en: Which is a fiction. I did ask him who his lawyers are, but the "or else" is Santos fantasy, perhaps his way of turning me into the menacing actor in all of this.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这是虚构的。我确实问过他的律师是谁，但“否则”是桑托斯的幻想，也许是他把我变成这一切中的威胁演员的方式。

contentParagraphs.en: Threats against journalists are unnervingly common. Most are flung by online trolls or aggressive attorneys and PR handlers trying to protect their clients.
contentParagraphs.zh: 针对记者的威胁非常普遍。大多数是由网络巨魔或激进的律师和公关人员试图保护他们的客户而抛出的。

contentParagraphs.en: At times, it can be hard to know when it's frivolous or hyperbole, or when you should take it seriously.
contentParagraphs.zh: 有时，很难知道什么时候是轻浮或夸张，什么时候应该认真对待。

contentParagraphs.en: I grappled with whether I should call this out at all. After all, drama and attention are oxygen to someone like Santos, who has become something of an internet troll since President Trump commuted his prison sentence, giving him his freedom back and his access to X.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我纠结着是否应该把这件事说出来。毕竟，对于像桑托斯这样的人来说，戏剧性和关注度就是氧气，自从特朗普总统减刑、重获自由并获得 X 的访问权后，桑托斯就成了互联网巨魔。

contentParagraphs.en: But given his mischaracterizations of how it at all went down, and his quick handiwork trying to cover his tracks, I thought it was worth setting the record straight.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但考虑到他对事情到底是如何发生的错误描述，以及他试图掩盖自己踪迹的快速行动，我认为值得澄清事实。

contentParagraphs.en: Since I published the story on the federal investigations into Santos, the Associated Press reported that Polymarket has cut ties with Santos, who had been paid by the company, a rival to Kalshi, to boost social media posts featuring some of its prediction markets.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自从我发表有关联邦调查桑托斯的报道后，美联社报道称，Polymarket 已与桑托斯断绝了联系，该公司是卡尔希的竞争对手，该公司向桑托斯支付报酬，以增加以其一些预测市场为主题的社交媒体帖子。

contentParagraphs.en: He also appears to be hoping to drum up some new business on Cameo, the site where celebrities are paid to record personalized videos.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他似乎还希望在 Cameo 上招揽一些新业务，这是一个向名人付费录制个性化视频的网站。

contentParagraphs.en: Santos is now offering 55% off his Cameo videos, meaning for $150, he will record himself saying just about anything. Although there's no guarantee he won't later deny it on X.
contentParagraphs.zh: Santos 现在为他的客串视频提供 55% 的折扣，这意味着只需 150 美元，他就能录制自己所说的任何内容。尽管不能保证他以后不会在 X 上否认这一点。

articles.title: How cellphone carriers prepare for hurricane season with AI, drones and 'cows'
articles.summary: Hurricane season is expected to be milder than usual this year. But that's not stopping cell phone companies from pulling out all the stops.
articles.titleZh: 手机运营商如何利用人工智能、无人机和“奶牛”为飓风季节做好准备
articles.summaryZh: 预计今年的飓风季节将比平常温和。但这并不能阻止手机公司使出浑身解数。

contentParagraphs.en: Residents make phone calls in their neighborhood after heavy rains from hurricane Helene caused record flooding and damage on September 28, 2024 in Asheville, North Carolina.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2024 年 9 月 28 日，北卡罗来纳州阿什维尔飓风海伦带来的大雨造成创纪录的洪水和破坏，居民们在附近打电话。

contentParagraphs.en: Melissa Sue Gerrits/Getty Images North America
contentParagraphs.zh: 梅丽莎·苏·格里茨/盖蒂图片社北美

contentParagraphs.en: Melissa Sue Gerrits/Getty Images North America
contentParagraphs.zh: 梅丽莎·苏·格里茨/盖蒂图片社北美

contentParagraphs.en: Anthony Leone and his wife, Corinne Saunders, have already started watching the weather. In a few weeks, they'll be filling coolers and plugging in generators. He has lived in North Carolina's Outer Banks for more than 20 years. So he's familiar with this routine that precedes the inevitable — hurricane season.
contentParagraphs.zh: 安东尼·莱昂内和他的妻子科琳·桑德斯已经开始观察天气。几周后，他们将填充冷却器并接通发电机。他已在北卡罗来纳州外滩群岛生活了 20 多年。所以他熟悉不可避免的飓风季节之前的惯例。

contentParagraphs.en: As for their cell phones? "We just plan ahead to not use [them]."
contentParagraphs.zh: 至于他们的手机？ “我们只是提前计划不使用[它们]。”

contentParagraphs.en: Hurricane season runs from June to November, and the National Weather Service said in May it's predicting below-normal activity in the Atlantic region for the first time since 2015. But the chance for at least one very powerful storm remains.
contentParagraphs.zh: 飓风季节从 6 月到 11 月，国家气象局 5 月表示，预计大西洋地区的活动将自 2015 年以来首次低于正常水平。但至少有可能发生一场非常强大的风暴。

contentParagraphs.en: Even though there may be slightly less hurricane activity this year, high winds and rain can still interrupt the power supply stationary cell towers rely on. As a result, cellphone carriers are fortifying their preparations with newer tools such as artificial intelligence and drones, so customers in the path of a hurricane can continue to communicate with loved ones, work from home and render aid.
contentParagraphs.zh: 尽管今年的飓风活动可能略有减少，但大风和降雨仍然会中断固定蜂窝塔所依赖的电力供应。因此，手机运营商正在利用人工智能和无人机等新工具加强准备工作，以便飓风路径上的客户可以继续与亲人沟通、在家工作并提供援助。

contentParagraphs.en: Anthony Leone and his wife, Corinne Saunders, kayak in Kitty Hawk Bay, in North Carolina's Outer Banks.
contentParagraphs.zh: 安东尼·莱昂内 (Anthony Leone) 和他的妻子科琳·桑德斯 (Corinne Saunders) 在北卡罗来纳州外滩群岛的基蒂霍克湾划皮划艇。

contentParagraphs.en: Anthony Leone/Anthony Leone
contentParagraphs.zh: 安东尼·莱昂/安东尼·莱昂

contentParagraphs.en: Anthony Leone/Anthony Leone
contentParagraphs.zh: 安东尼·莱昂/安东尼·莱昂

contentParagraphs.en: Strategies the companies are using
contentParagraphs.zh: 公司正在使用的策略

contentParagraphs.en: Verizon Wireless starts its hurricane response plans about a week before one is forecasted in an area, according to Srini Kalapala, Verizon's senior vice president of wireless engineering and operations. Before a storm, Verizon uses drones to take pictures of a coverage area. It then takes more pictures after a storm to assess the damage. AI sends a precise location of the damage to engineers and technicians as they start their repairs.
contentParagraphs.zh: Verizon 无线工程和运营高级副总裁斯里尼·卡拉帕拉 (Srini Kalapala) 表示，Verizon Wireless 在预计某个地区将出现飓风之前大约一周就启动了飓风应对计划。在暴风雨来临之前，Verizon 使用无人机拍摄覆盖区域的照片。然后，它会在暴风雨过后拍摄更多照片来评估损失。当工程师和技术人员开始维修时，人工智能会向他们发送损坏的精确位置。

contentParagraphs.en: "Hurricanes, especially — you don't understand the damage until it's fully done," Kalapala said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 卡拉帕拉说：“尤其是飓风，只有在它完全造成损害之前，你才会了解其造成的损害。”

contentParagraphs.en: Verizon has an assortment of mobile tools should the company's stationary cell towers give out during a hurricane. Many of them have animals for codenames; for example, COW stands for "cell on wheels," and a HAWK, or "high altitude wireless kennewhat," is a drone that can carry small towers to provide service from the sky. Kalapala said these tools have helped shave restoration times down to minutes and hours.
contentParagraphs.zh: Verizon 拥有各种移动工具，以防该公司的固定手机信号塔在飓风期间失效。其中许多都以动物为代号；例如，COW 代表“轮子上的单元”，HAWK 或“高空无线肯尼瓦”是一种可以携带小型塔从空中提供服务的无人机。卡拉帕拉表示，这些工具帮助将恢复时间缩短至几分钟和几小时。

contentParagraphs.en: AT&T also makes preparations about a week before a hurricane. Its disaster response fleet includes boats, barges and helicopters that can transport team members and equipment through hard-to-reach areas including swamps and mountains after a disaster, according to Shannon Browning, the associate director of AT&T's Network Disaster Recovery team. The company will often move equipment across the country as needed throughout the year, for events such as hurricanes, snowstorms and wildfires, he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: AT&T 也在飓风来临前一周左右做好准备。 AT&T 网络灾难恢复团队副总监香农·布朗宁 (Shannon Browning) 表示，其救灾船队包括船只、驳船和直升机，可以在灾难发生后运送团队成员和设备穿越沼泽和山区等难以到达的地区。他说，该公司经常会根据全年需要在全国范围内转移设备，以应对飓风、暴风雪和野火等事件。

contentParagraphs.en: "It used to be [that hurricanes] were very season-specific. And then you started to see that wildfire season crept into hurricane season," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “过去，飓风是有特定季节的。然后你开始看到野火季节悄悄进入了飓风季节，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: As a result, the company has merged several of its disaster teams into one group, which has helped the company streamline its approach to public safety, Browning said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 布朗宁说，因此，该公司已将多个灾难小组合并为一个小组，这有助于该公司简化其公共安全方法。

contentParagraphs.en: Jon Freier, T-Mobile's chief operating officer, said the company prepares for disasters three to five months in advance.
contentParagraphs.zh: T-Mobile 首席运营官 Jon Freier 表示，公司提前三到五个月为灾难做好准备。

contentParagraphs.en: If the power goes out during a storm, it can take a few hours or days to restore service. In the meantime, customers get free satellite service, Freier said, and the company uses AI tools to automatically adjust antennas and conserve backup batteries when needed, which stretches the time period that they can be used.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果暴风雨期间停电，可能需要几个小时或几天才能恢复服务。弗雷尔表示，与此同时，客户可以获得免费的卫星服务，公司使用人工智能工具在需要时自动调整天线并节省备用电池，从而延长了它们的使用时间。

contentParagraphs.en: "From my experience in these disasters, people will go without power a little bit longer, and they're more patient," Freier said. "But they want that smartphone to work, and to be connected."
contentParagraphs.zh: 弗雷尔说：“根据我在这些灾难中的经验，人们失去电力的时间会更长一些，而且他们会更有耐心。” “但他们希望智能手机能够工作并联网。”

contentParagraphs.en: What some users have experienced
contentParagraphs.zh: 一些用户的经历

contentParagraphs.en: Leone, the North Carolina resident, has had several cell phone providers over the years, with different levels of performance during hurricanes and other major weather events. With his last carrier, getting regular service was a year-round issue; he could only get a signal on highways and at the beach. He has now been with AT&T for more than a decade, and he said his service has been fairly reliable during hurricanes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 北卡罗来纳州居民莱昂内多年来拥有多家手机提供商，在飓风和其他重大天气事件期间表现各异。对于他的最后一家航空公司来说，获得定期服务是一个全年的问题。他只能在高速公路和海滩上收到信号。他现在已经在 AT&T 工作了十多年，他说他的服务在飓风期间相当可靠。

contentParagraphs.en: "We usually get a text message saying, 'Hey, there's a storm coming,'" and if he loses service in a power outage, "It's only a couple days, usually, before they fix it."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们通常会收到一条短信说，‘嘿，暴风雨即将来临’”，如果他因停电而失去服务，“通常只需几天，他们就会修复它。”

contentParagraphs.en: But before civilians' smartphones power up again, first responders' needs have to be met. Many major companies prioritize and tailor networks specifically for first responders — including law enforcement, firefighters and paramedics.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但在平民的智能手机再次启动之前，急救人员的需求必须得到满足。许多大公司专门为急救人员（包括执法人员、消防员和护理人员）优先考虑并定制网络。

contentParagraphs.en: Peter Antevy is the medical director for several fire departments in Broward County, Fla.. He said his teams rely on carriers' cell and Wi-Fi services not just to make voice calls, but to conduct telemedicine appointments, send test results and transmit live updates from 911 dispatch centers to ambulances.
contentParagraphs.zh: Peter Antevy 是佛罗里达州布劳沃德县多个消防部门的医疗主任。他表示，他的团队不仅依靠运营商的手机和 Wi-Fi 服务进行语音通话，还进行远程医疗预约、发送测试结果以及将实时更新从 911 调度中心传输到救护车。

contentParagraphs.en: "There is a lot of data that goes back and forth," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “有很多数据来回流动，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: So it's important for company representatives to be in the room during local disaster response strategy meetings. Amy Weber, the chief of emergency medical services at the Galveston County Health District in Texas, said representatives from Verizon and AT&T come to her department's meetings to plan what equipment they'll send, and when.
contentParagraphs.zh: 因此，在当地灾难应对策略会议期间，公司代表出席会议非常重要。德克萨斯州加尔维斯顿县卫生区紧急医疗服务主管艾米·韦伯 (Amy Weber) 表示，Verizon 和 AT&T 的代表参加了她部门的会议，计划他们将发送哪些设备以及何时发送。

contentParagraphs.en: "Communication is always a huge breakdown for first responders, because we get inundated with calls just from the service area, so putting us at top priority helps us be able to do our job," Weber said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 韦伯说：“对于急救人员来说，沟通始终是一个巨大的障碍，因为我们会被来自服务区的电话淹没，因此将我们置于首要位置有助于我们完成工作。”

contentParagraphs.en: Jackie Santillan, a doctoral student and content creator, said with her old provider, there was just one square foot of her home in the Houston suburbs where she could make a call. With her current provider, T-Mobile, she says not much has changed. She
contentParagraphs.zh: 博士生兼内容创作者杰基·桑蒂兰 (Jackie Santillan) 表示，她在休斯敦郊区的家中只有一平方英尺的空间可以打电话。她表示，她目前的提供商 T-Mobile 并没有发生太大变化。她

articles.title: US visa rejections, war on Iran keep fans away from World Cup
articles.summary: High visa application fees, equally high rejection rates, an unwelcoming America, and the war combine to deter fans.
articles.titleZh: 美国签证被拒、伊朗战争让球迷远离世界杯
articles.summaryZh: 高额的签证申请费、同样高的拒绝率、不受欢迎的美国以及战争，这些因素结合起来让球迷望而却步。

contentParagraphs.en: When Iran qualified for the FIFA World Cup last March, the men’s national team didn’t expect their participation to hinge on visas being granted by hosts, the United States, only at the last moment – if at all.Nor did Iranian fans eager to support Team Melli expect to be banned from entry by the US. President Donald Trump signed an executive order last June halting visa issuance to a handful of countries, including Iran, which the US designated a “state sponsor of terrorism”.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Norwegian FA supports ethics complaint against FIFA chief Infantinolist 2 of 4From Yildiz to Diomande: Top 10 rising stars to watch at the World Cuplist 3 of 4US artist sues FIFA over destruction of Dallas whale mural for World Cuplist 4 of 4All to know about the FIFA World Cup 2026 stadiumsend of listPerhaps most unanticipated for Iranians was that the host nation of the largest sporting event in the world would launch a war on their country just months before the tournament began.For Amir Ghalenoei’s side, the joint US-Israel war was more than a wrench thrown into World Cup preparation plans; it was tangible and personal, as thousands across the country were killed by missile attacks.It was the US bombing Azadi Stadium, home to several local matches and where the national team trained. It was the men’s team holding tiny backpacks in remembrance of the students massacred in a US strike on a school in Minab the day the war began.Iran’s Milad Mohammadi, Hossein Kanaani, Shoja Khalilzadeh, Alireza Beiranvand and Mehdi Taremi hold schoolbags in memory of the victims of the girls’ school bombing in Minab, Iran, as they line up with the match officials and the Nigerian players before the friendly match in Mardan Sports Complex, Antalya, Turkiye, March 27, 2026 [Umit Bektas/Reuters]After months of politically charged rigmarole between the US and Iran – which led to them switching basecamps to Mexico instead – the men’s national football team will find themselves playing in the shadow of war. That too, if the US grants them visas in time.For Iranian football fans, travelling to the US was “almost impossible” even without the visa challenges or the war. There are no direct means of transport between the countries, which do not have formal diplomatic relations.“Aside from the visa issue, you have to take two- or three-way routes from Tehran to get to the US,” said Ali, a fan who did not want to share his full name for safety reasons.“Returning from the US to Iran is a big challenge in itself, with the possibility of being arrested by the [Iranian] government,” he added. The war has increased scrutiny of antinational sentiment within Iran, resulting in executions of people arrested on accusations of spying for Israel or the US.Political repercussions extend to the sport sphere, too. Iran’s top footballer Sardar Azmoun was expelled from the national team in March for a perceived act of disloyalty to the government, when he posted a picture on social media of a ⁠meeting with Dubai ruler Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum. Relations between the UAE and Iran have been tense during the war, with Iran hitting the Emirates repeatedly and accusing it of allowing the US to use its territory for attacks on Iran.The US war on Iran, now nearing its 100th day, has also deterred fans globally from attending the World Cup.“Football is called the Beautiful Game for a reason, for its ability to unite people,” South African football fan Byron Pillay told Al Jazeera.“But it’s hard to believe in that magic with the politics and war rhetoric off the field of play, specially when one of the tournament hosts is central to that.”Compatriot Riaz Hamed echoed those reservations. “With the stance of America in particular, regarding the treatment of fans and immigrants in the country, I don’t believe it to be entirely safe to attend.”Fears have been stoked by reports from organisations such as Human Rights Watch, which said an asylum seeker who attended the Club World Cup final last year in New Jersey with his children was arrested by the Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) department and deported to his country of origin.Khayran Noor, an international sports lawyer based in Kenya, emphasised that sport cannot be separated from wider geopolitical dimensions.“If participation can be shaped by geopolitical realities outside the game itself, does that ultimately undermine the inclusive ideals these tournaments claim to represent?” Noor said in an interview with Al Jazeera.“Football is global, but global mobility is not; the World Cup sits directly at the intersection of that contradiction.”Mounting visa rejections have also spooked fans from attempting to attend the World Cup.The US has launched a FIFA Priority Appointment Scheduling System (PASS), which expedites visa interviews for fans who have bought tickets through FIFA. But it does not guarantee a visa.Last month, a group of nearly 150 Ghana football fans saw their visa applications rejected.Godwin Nii Armah, 32, scrapped his travel plans for the World Cup for personal reasons, but knew he might have shared the same fate as those compatriots. He also admitted that travelling to Toronto, Boston and Philadelphia to support the Black Stars would have been a costly logistical headache in addition to international flights and visa fees.Ghana nationals have to pay a $185 fee with their US visa application and 100 Canadian dollars ($71) for the Canadian visa. Add the two, and the amount is comparable to the monthly per capita income in Ghana.Noor questioned whether future FIFA host agreements should include obligations relating to accessibility and mobility before hosting rights are awarded.“If teams and fans from particular parts of the world face structural barriers before they can even attend, then the broader spirit of inclusion that these tournaments seek to embody risks being undermined.”She acknowledged that while states understandably retain sovereign r
contentParagraphs.zh: 当伊朗去年三月获得世界杯参赛资格时，伊朗男足国家队并没有想到他们的参赛资格会取决于东道主美国在最后一刻发放的签证——如果有的话。渴望支持梅利队的伊朗球迷也没有想到会被美国禁止入境。唐纳德·特朗普总统于去年 6 月签署了一项行政命令，停止向包括伊朗在内的少数国家发放签证，美国将其认定为“恐怖主义国家支持者”。 推荐故事列表 4 项列表 1 of 4 挪威足协支持对国际足联主席 Infantinolist 的道德投诉 2 of 4 从 Yildiz 到 Diomande：世界杯名单上最值得关注的 10 位新星 3 of 4 美国艺术家起诉国际足联因破坏世界杯名单上的达拉斯鲸鱼壁画4 of 4关于 2026 年 FIFA 世界杯体育场馆的所有信息发送列表结束也许伊朗人最意想不到的是，世界上最大的体育赛事的主办国将在比赛开始前几个月对自己的国家发动战争。这是有形的、针对个人的，因为全国各地有数千人因导弹袭击而丧生。那是美国轰炸的阿扎迪体育场，这里是几场当地比赛的主场，也是国家队训练的地方。战争开始当天，男子队背着小背包，纪念在美国对米纳布一所学校的袭击中惨遭屠杀的学生。伊朗队球员米拉德·穆罕默迪、侯赛因·卡纳尼、绍贾·哈利勒扎德、阿里雷扎·贝兰万德和迈赫迪·塔雷米在比赛开始前与比赛官员和尼日利亚球员排队，背着书包纪念伊朗米纳布女子学校爆炸事件的受害者。 2026 年 3 月 27 日在土耳其安塔利亚马尔丹体育中心举行的友谊赛 [Umit Bektas/Reuters] 经过数月美国和伊朗之间充满政治色彩的争论 — — 导致他们将大本营改到墨西哥 — — 国家男子足球队将发现自己在战争的阴影下比赛。如果美国及时向他们发放签证，情况也是如此。对于伊朗球迷来说，即使没有签证挑战或战争，前往美国“几乎是不可能的”。这两个没有正式外交关系的国家之间没有直接的交通工具。“除了签证问题，你必须从德黑兰走两到三程路线才能到达美国，”出于安全原因不愿透露全名的球迷阿里说。“从美国返回伊朗本身就是一个很大的挑战，有可能被[伊朗]政府逮捕，”他补充道。这场战争加强了对伊朗境内反民族情绪的审查，导致一些因被指控为以色列或美国从事间谍活动而被捕的人被处决。政治影响也延伸到了体育领域。伊朗顶级足球运动员萨达尔·阿兹蒙 (Sardar Azmoun) 三月份因被认为对政府不忠而被开除出国家队，当时他在社交媒体上发布了一张与迪拜统治者穆罕默德·本·拉希德·阿勒马克图姆 (Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum) 会面的照片。战争期间，阿联酋和伊朗之间的关系一直紧张，伊朗多次袭击阿联酋，并指责其允许美国利用其领土袭击伊朗。美国对伊朗的战争现已接近第 100 天，也阻止了全球球迷观看世界杯。南非足球迷拜伦·皮莱告诉半岛电视台：“足球被称为美丽的运动是有原因的，因为它能够团结人民。”赛场外的政治和战争言论，特别是当赛事东道主之一是其中的核心时。”同胞里亚兹·哈米德（Riaz Hamed）回应了这些保留意见。 “特别是美国在国内球迷和移民待遇问题上的立场，我认为参加比赛并不完全安全。”人权观察等组织的报道加剧了人们的担忧，该组织称，一名去年带着孩子参加新泽西州世俱杯决赛的寻求庇护者被移民和海关执法局（ICE）逮捕，并被驱逐回原籍国。 肯尼亚国际体育律师海兰·努尔（Khayran Noor）强调，体育运动不能与更广泛的地缘政治维度分开。“如果比赛本身之外的地缘政治现实可以影响参与度，那么这是否最终会破坏这些锦标赛声称代表的包容性理想？”努尔在接受半岛电视台采访时表示，“足球是全球性的，但全球流动性却不然；世界杯正好处于这一矛盾的交汇点。”越来越多的签证被拒也让试图参加世界杯的球迷感到害怕。美国推出了国际足联优先预约安排系统（PASS），可以为通过国际足联购买门票的球迷加快签证面试速度。但这并不能保证获得签证。上个月，一群近150名加纳球迷的签证申请被拒绝。32岁的戈德温·尼·阿尔玛(Godwin Nii Armah)因个人原因取消了世界杯旅行计划，但他知道自己可能和这些同胞有同样的命运。他还承认，除了国际航班和签证费外，前往多伦多、波士顿和费城支持黑星队还会带来昂贵的后勤费用。加纳国民必须在申请美国签证时支付 185 美元的费用，并为加拿大签证支付 100 加元（71 美元）的费用。将两者相加，这一数额与加纳的人均月收入相当。努尔质疑未来的国际足联主办协议是否应在授予主办权之前包括与无障碍和流动性相关的义务。“如果来自世界特定地区的球队和球迷在参加比赛之前面临结构性障碍，那么这些赛事所寻求体现的更广泛的包容精神将面临被破坏的风险。”她承认，尽管各国保留主权是可以理解的。
